---
applyTo: "packages/genui/ui-judge/**/*"
---

Keep UI Judge a Rust-only library crate. Its public API, model integration, tests, and user examples belong under `packages/genui/ui-judge`; do not add a binary target, CLI, npm package manifest, TypeScript facade, JavaScript test suite, or server process adapter back to this directory. The existing ReactLynx fixture source is a runtime asset consumed by the repository's headless runner, not a JavaScript UI Judge API or test harness.

Keep the crate-root API restricted to `judge_page`, `JudgePageRequest`, `UiJudgeResult`, and `UiJudgeError`. The request uses only standard-library field types and always performs visual-correctness scoring internally. Do not export model clients, model options, runner types, screenshot helpers, prompts, parsers, GEQI dimension types, fixture helpers, or screenshot-comparison APIs.

Keep reference-image comparison inside the Rust crate and reachable through `JudgePageRequest::reference_image`. Preserve `reference` as textual model context; accept reference images as plain base64, base64 data URLs, or HTTP(S) URLs, with explicit compressed-byte, decoded-dimension, pixel-count, and allocation limits. After the final screenshot is captured, run the VLM and reference-image comparison as independent chains that share only the captured PNG. The VLM always scores that single screenshot from `task` and textual `reference`; never send the reference image, alignment result, pixel diff, or deterministic similarity to the VLM. The reference-image chain must only load and normalize the baseline, align it with normalized cross-correlation, and compute block-based pixel differences and a diff image. Run bounded alignment and pixel-diff work off the current-thread runtime. Keep `score`, `reason`, and `summary` owned by the VLM; use `error` for the primary page-capture or VLM chain. Report reference-image failures separately without overwriting VLM output, and preserve successful comparison diagnostics when the VLM fails. Expose only standard-type diagnostics on `UiJudgeResult`; keep alignment, comparison, artifact, and algorithm types private to the crate.

Preserve the former TypeScript reference-comparison semantics during migration work: successful alignment compares the overlap crop, fallback comparison normalizes both images to their minimum dimensions with fill-style resizing, and pixel distance compares all four raw RGBA channels. Penalizing non-overlap or premultiplying alpha is a separate metric change, not a migration cleanup. Keep CPU-heavy image work on Tokio's blocking pool behind a small shared semaphore; move each permit into the worker so timeout or cancellation cannot release capacity before the worker exits, and use cooperative cancellation checks in loops owned by UI Judge.

Do not modify or duplicate `lynx-headless-rust-test-runner` as part of UI Judge work. `judge_page` must accept only `file://`, `http://`, and `https://` URLs; reject bare paths and other schemes before initializing the model or headless runtime. Internally use the runner's existing navigation, DOM, selector tap, wait, and software screenshot APIs. Keep model creation, the headless Lynx connection, and the page lifecycle inside `judge_page`. Do not re-export `Lynx`, `Page`, or other runner implementation types or require callers to construct them. Keep `judge_page` compatible with sequential use on a Tokio current-thread runtime because the runner's native task pump and page state are bound to their creation thread. Preserve natural-language `steps` by using Agent SDK to choose selector taps and waits from the current DOM and screenshot; report swipe, scrolling, typing, and coordinate touch as unsupported because the existing runner does not expose them. Do not add Kitten-Lynx, Android, ADB, an emulator, Playwright, or a browser as a UI Judge runtime dependency.

Keep `JudgePageRequest::timeout` compatible with the former TypeScript API: it is a per-operation and per-step limit, not an overall request deadline. Connection, navigation, each natural-language step, final screenshot capture, VLM scoring, and optional reference-image comparison each receive the full duration; document that distinction wherever the request API is shown.

Use Agent SDK for structured VLM evaluation. Keep any OpenAI-compatible transport adapter in Rust and preserve the established `MIDSCENE_MODEL_*`, `MIDSCENE_MODEL_INIT_CONFIG_JSON`, legacy `MIDSCENE_OPENAI_INIT_CONFIG_JSON`, OpenAI aliases (including `OPENAI_ORG_ID` and `OPENAI_PROJECT_ID`), and timeout environment-variable inputs for configuration compatibility; those names do not permit a Midscene runtime dependency. Keep unit tests deterministic with `UI_JUDGE_MODEL_RESPONSE_JSON`. The runtime-backed `headless_e2e` integration test is intentionally different: it must reject mock-response variables unconditionally. It must skip inside the test when all supported real credential environment variables are absent or empty; otherwise it must call the real model supplied through the injected model environment.

Keep 0-5 score normalization and typed page result structures in the Rust library. Do not add result-file generation, a UI Judge PR-comment action, or a GitHub comment publishing workflow.

Keep the existing React Lynx fixture at `packages/genui/ui-judge/tests/fixtures/react`, because the unchanged headless runner reads that path. Build its `.lynx.bundle` into the ignored `.generated/` directory and never commit generated bundle binaries.
